paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Silk is a fine, smooth material produced from the cocoons - soft protective shells - that are made by mulberry silkworms (insect larvae). Legend has it that it was Lei Tzu, wife of the Yellow Emperor, ruler of China in about 3000 BC, who discovered silkworms. One account of the story goes that as she was taking a walk in her husband's gardens, she discovered that silkworms were responsible for the destruction of several mulberry trees. She collected a number of cocoons and sat down to have a rest. It just so happened that while she was sipping some tea, one of the cocoons that she had collected landed in the hot tea and started to unravel into a fine thread. Lei Tzu found that she could wind this thread around her fingers. Subsequently, she persuaded her husband to allow her to rear silkworms on a grove of mulberry trees. She also devised a special reel to draw the fibres from the cocoon into a single thread so that they would be strong enough to be woven into fabric. While it is unknown just how much of this is true, it is certainly known that silk cultivation has existed in China for several millennia.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Originally, silkworm farming was solely restricted to women, and it was they who were responsible for the growing, harvesting and weaving. Silk quickly grew into a symbol of status, and originally only royalty were entitled to have clothes made of silk. The rules were gradually relaxed over the years until finally during the Qing Dynasty (1644-1911 AD), even peasants, the lowest caste, were also entitled to wear silk. Sometime during the Han Dynasty (206 BC-220 AD), silk was so prized that it was also used as a unit of currency. Government officials were paid their salary in silk, and farmers paid their taxes in grain and silk. Silk was also used as diplomatic gifts by the emperor. Fishing lines, bowstrings, musical instruments and paper were all made using silk. The earliest indication of silk paper being used was discovered in the tomb of a noble who is estimated to have died around 168 AD.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Demand for this exotic fabric eventually created the lucrative trade route now known as the Silk Road, taking silk westward and bringing gold, silver and wool to the East. It was named the Silk Road after its most precious commodity, which was considered to be worth more than gold. The Silk Road stretched over 6,000 kilometres from Eastern China to the Mediterranean Sea, following the Great Wall of China, climbing the Pamir mountain range, crossing modern-day Afghanistan and going on to the Middle East, with a major trading market in Damascus. From there, the merchandise was shipped across the Mediterranean Sea. Few merchants travelled the entire route; goods were handled mostly by a series of middlemen.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: With the mulberry silkworm being native to China, the country was the world's sole producer of silk for many hundreds of years. The secret of silk-making eventually reached the rest of the world via the Byzantine Empire, which ruled over the Mediterranean region of southern Europe, North Africa and the Middle East during the period 330-1453 AD. According to another legend, monks working for the Byzantine emperor Justinian smuggled silkworm eggs to Constantinople (Istanbul in modern-day Turkey) in 550 AD, concealed inside hollow bamboo walking canes. The Byzantines were as secretive as the Chinese, however, and for many centuries the weaving and trading of silk fabric was a strict imperial monopoly. Then in the seventh century, the Arabs conquered Persia, capturing their magnificent silks in the process. Silk production thus spread through Africa, Sicily and Spain as the Arabs swept through these lands. Andalusia in southern Spain was Europe's main silk-producing centre in the tenth century. By the thirteenth century, however, Italy had become Europe's leader in silk production and export. Venetian merchants traded extensively in silk and encouraged silk growers to settle in Italy. Even now, silk processed in the province of Como in northern Italy enjoys an esteemed reputation.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The nineteenth century and industrialisation saw the downfall of the European silk industry. Cheaper Japanese silk, trade in which was greatly facilitated by the opening of the Suez Canal, was one of the many factors driving the trend. Then in the twentieth century, new manmade fibres, such as nylon, started to be used in what had traditionally been silk products, such as stockings and parachutes. The two world wars, which interrupted the supply of raw material from Japan, also stifled the European silk industry. After the Second World War, Japan's silk production was restored, with improved production and quality of raw silk. Japan was to remain the world's biggest producer of raw silk, and practically the only major exporter of raw silk, until the 1970s. However, in more recent decades, China has gradually recaptured its position as the world's biggest producer and exporter of raw silk and silk yarn. Today, around 125,000 metric tons of silk are produced in the world, and almost two thirds of that production takes place in China.

questions.title: The Byzantines spread the practice of silk production across the West.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Animal migration, however it is defined, is far more than just the movement of animals. It can loosely be described as travel that takes place at regular intervals—often in an annual cycle—that may involve many members of a species, and is rewarded only after a long journey. It suggests inherited instinct. The biologist Hugh Dingle has identified five characteristics that apply, in varying degrees and combinations, to all migrations. They are prolonged movements that carry animals outside familiar habitats; they tend to be linear, not zigzaggy; they involve special behaviours concerning preparation (such as overfeeding) and arrival; they demand special allocations of energy. And one more: migrating animals maintain an intense attentiveness to the greater mission, which keeps them undistracted by temptations and undeterred by challenges that would turn other animals aside.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: An arctic tern, on its 20,000 km flight from the extreme south of South America to the Arctic Circle, will take no notice of a nice smelly herring offered from a bird-watcher's boat along the way. While local gulls will dive voraciously for such handouts, the tern flies on. Why? The arctic tern resists distraction because it is driven at that moment by an instinctive sense of something we humans find admirable: larger purpose. In other words, it is determined to reach its destination. The bird senses that it can eat, rest and mate later. Right now it is totally focused on the journey; its undivided intent is arrival. Reaching some gravelly coastline in the Arctic, upon which other arctic terns have converged, will serve its larger purpose as shaped by evolution: finding a place, a time, and a set of circumstances in which it can successfully hatch and rear offspring.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: But migration is a complex issue, and biologists define it differently, depending in part on what sorts of animals they study. Joel Berger, of the University of Montana, who works on the American pronghorn and other large terrestrial mammals, prefers what he calls a simple, practical definition suited to his beasts: 'movements from a seasonal home area away to another home area and back again'. Generally the reason for such seasonal back-and-forth movement is to seek resources that aren't available within a single area year-round.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: But daily vertical movements by zooplankton in the ocean—upward by night to seek food, downward by day to escape predators—can also be considered migration. So can the movement of aphids when, having depleted the young leaves on one food plant, their offspring then fly onward to a different host plant, with no one aphid ever returning to where it started.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Dingle is an evolutionary biologist who studies insects. His definition is more intricate than Berger's, citing those five features that distinguish migration from other forms of movement. They allow for the fact that, for example, aphids will become sensitive to blue light (from the sky) when it's time for takeoff on their big journey, and sensitive to yellow light (reflected from tender young leaves) when it's appropriate to land. Birds will fatten themselves with heavy feeding in advance of a long migrational flight. The value of his definition, Dingle argues, is that it focuses attention on what the phenomenon of wildebeest migration shares with the phenomenon of the aphids, and therefore helps guide researchers towards understanding how evolution has produced them all.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Human behaviour, however, is having a detrimental impact on animal migration. The pronghorn, which resembles an antelope, though they are unrelated, is the fastest land mammal of the New World. One population, which spends the summer in the mountainous Grand Teton National Park of the western USA, follows a narrow route from its summer range in the mountains, across a river, and down onto the plains. Here they wait out the frozen months, feeding mainly on sagebrush blown clear of snow. These pronghorn are notable for the invariance of their migration route and the severity of its constriction at three bottlenecks. If they can't pass through each of the three during their spring migration, they can't reach their bounty of summer grazing; if they can't pass through again in autumn, escaping south onto those windblown plains, they are likely to die trying to overwinter in the deep snow. Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators, traverse high, open shoulders of land, where they can see and run. At one of the bottlenecks, forested hills rise to form a V, leaving a corridor of open ground only about 150 metres wide, filled with private homes. Increasing development is leading toward a crisis for the pronghorn, threatening to choke off their passageway.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Conservation scientists, along with some biologists and land managers within the USA's National Park Service and other agencies, are now working to preserve migrational behaviours, not just species and habitats. A National Forest has recognised the path of the pronghorn, much of which passes across its land, as a protected migration corridor. But neither the Forest Service nor the Park Service can control what happens on private land at a bottleneck. And with certain other migrating species, the challenge is complicated further—by vastly greater distances traversed, more jurisdictions, more borders, more dangers along the way. We will require wisdom and resoluteness to ensure that migrating species can continue their journeying a while longer.

NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: Local gulls and migrating arctic terns behave in the same way when offered food.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“Local gulls and migrating arctic terns behave in the same way when offered food.”，意思是“本地海鸥和迁徙的北极燕鸥在被提供食物时行为相同”。然而，原文明确指出本地海鸥会贪婪地俯冲去抢食物，而北极燕鸥则会忽视食物继续飞行。因此，题干中的信息与原文内容相反，正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: While local gulls will dive voraciously for such handouts, the tern flies on.
central_sentences.translation: 当地海鸥会贪婪地俯冲抢食这些施舍的食物，而燕鸥则继续飞行。

target_sen.target_sen: While local gulls will dive voraciously for such handouts, the tern flies on.
An arctic tern, on its 20,000 km flight from the extreme south of South America to the Arctic circle, will take no notice of a nice smelly herring offered from a bird-watcher's boat along the way.
The arctic tern resists distraction because it is driven at that moment by an instinctive sense of something we humans find admirable: larger purpose.

Reaching some gravelly coastline in the Arctic, upon which other arctic terns have converged, will serve its larger purpose as shaped by evolution: finding a place, a time, and a set of circumstances in which it can successfully hatch and rear offspring.
While local gulls will dive voraciously for such handouts, the tern flies on.
While local gulls will dive voraciously for such handouts, the tern flies on.
An arctic tern, on its 20,000 km flight from the extreme south of South America to the Arctic circle, will take no notice of a nice smelly herring offered from a bird-watcher's boat along the way.
While local gulls will dive voraciously for such handouts, the tern flies on.

questions.title: Experts' definitions of migration tend to vary according to their area of study.
questions.analysis: 这道题的答案是TRUE，因为文章明确提到“biologists define it differently, depending in part on what sorts of animals they study”，这与题干中的“Experts' definitions of migration tend to vary according to their area of study”意思完全一致。题干中的“Experts' definitions of migration”对应文章中的“biologists define it differently”，“tend to vary”对应“depending in part”，“their area of study”对应“what sorts of animals they study”。因此，题干信息在文章中可以找到，答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: But migration is a complex issue, and biologists define it differently, depending in part on what sorts of animals they study.
central_sentences.translation: 但迁徙是一个复杂的问题，生物学家对它的定义各不相同，部分取决于他们研究的动物种类。

target_sen.target_sen: But migration is a complex issue, and biologists define it differently, depending in part on what sorts of animals they study.
But migration is a complex issue, and biologists define it differently, depending in part on what sorts of animals they study.

syn_ary.synonym: what sorts of animals they study

target_sen.target_sen: But migration is a complex issue, and biologists define it differently, depending in part on what sorts of animals they study.

questions.title: Very few experts agree that the movement of aphids can be considered migration.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“very few experts agree”在文章中并没有提到，也没有提到有多少专家同意或不同意这个观点。因此，正确答案是 NOT GIVEN。文章中只是说明了蚜虫的移动可以被认为是迁徙，但没有提到专家的数量或意见分歧。

central_sentences.sentence: So can the movement of aphids when, having depleted the young leaves on one food plant, their offspring then fly onward to a different host plant, with no one aphid ever returning to where it started.
central_sentences.translation: 同样，当蚜虫耗尽一种食物植物的嫩叶后，其后代飞往另一种寄主植物的移动也可以被认为是迁徙，没有一只蚜虫会返回它开始的地方。

target_sen.target_sen: So can the movement of aphids when, having depleted the young leaves on one food plant, their offspring then fly onward to a different host plant, with no one aphid ever returning to where it started.
But daily vertical movements by zooplankton in the ocean - upward by night to seek food, downward by day to escape predators - can also be considered migration.

questions.title: Aphids' journeys are affected by changes in the light that they perceive.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“aphids' journeys”对应原文中的“aphids”和“journey”，而“changes in the light”对应原文中的“blue light”和“yellow light”。原文明确提到蚜虫在迁徙过程中对不同光线的敏感性，这表明它们的迁徙行为确实受到光线变化的影响。因此，答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Dingle is an evolutionary biologist who studies insects. His definition is more intricate than Berger's, citing those five features that distinguish migration from other forms of movement. They allow for the fact that, for example, aphids will become sensitive to blue light (from the sky) when it's time for takeoff on their big journey, and sensitive to yellow light (reflected from tender young leaves) when it's appropriate to land.
central_sentences.translation: 丁格尔是一位研究昆虫的进化生物学家。他的定义比伯格的更为复杂，提出了五个特征来区分迁徙和其他形式的移动。这些特征考虑到了一些事实，例如，蚜虫在准备起飞进行大迁徙时会对蓝光（来自天空）敏感，而在适合降落时会对黄光（从嫩叶反射）敏感。

target_sen.target_sen: They allow for the fact that, for example, aphids will become sensitive to blue light (from the sky) when it's time for takeoff on their big journey, and sensitive to yellow light (reflected from tender young leaves) when it's appropriate to land.

They allow for the fact that, for example, aphids will become sensitive to blue light (from the sky) when it's time for takeoff on their big journey, and sensitive to yellow light (reflected from tender young leaves) when it's appropriate to land.

locating_words.locating_word: affected by changes in the light

questions.title: Dingle's aim is to distinguish between the migratory behaviours of different species.
questions.analysis: 题干中的陈述是“Dingle's aim is to distinguish between the migratory behaviours of different species.”，意思是Dingle的目标是区分不同物种的迁徙行为。然而，原文中提到Dingle的目标是通过定义迁徙行为的共同特征来帮助研究人员理解进化如何产生这些行为，而不是区分不同物种的迁徙行为。因此，题干中的陈述与原文内容相反，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: The value of his definition, Dingle argues, is that it focuses attention on what the phenomenon of wildebeest migration shares with the phenomenon of the aphids, and therefore helps guide researchers towards understanding how evolution has produced them all.
central_sentences.translation: Dingle认为，他的定义的价值在于它关注野牛迁徙现象与蚜虫现象的共同之处，因此有助于引导研究人员理解进化是如何产生这些现象的。

syn_ary.synonym: The value of his definition, Dingle argues

target_sen.target_sen: The value of his definition, Dingle argues, is that it focuses attention on what the phenomenon of wildebeest migration shares with the phenomenon of the aphids, and therefore helps guide researchers towards understanding how evolution has produced them all.
His definition is more intricate than Berger's, citing those five features that distinguish migration from other forms of movement.
His definition is more intricate than Berger's, citing those five features that distinguish migration from other forms of movement.
Birds will fatten themselves with heavy feeding in advance of a long migrational flight.

The value of his definition, Dingle argues, is that it focuses attention on what the phenomenon of wildebeest migration shares with the phenomenon of the aphids, and therefore helps guide researchers towards understanding how evolution has produced them all.

The value of his definition, Dingle argues, is that it focuses attention on what the phenomenon of wildebeest migration shares with the phenomenon of the aphids, and therefore helps guide researchers towards understanding how evolution has produced them all.

groups.desc:
Questions 19-22
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: travel on open land where they can look out for predators.
eat more than they need for immediate purposes.
be governed by the availability of water.

questions.title: According to Dingle, migratory routes are likely to
questions.analysis: 根据文章内容，Dingle 提到迁徙路线的一个特征是 "they tend to be linear, not zigzaggy"，即它们倾向于直线，而不是曲折的。这与选项 G: follow a straight line 完全对应。因此，正确答案是 G。

central_sentences.sentence: They are prolonged movements that carry animals outside familiar habitats; they tend to be linear, not zigzaggy;
central_sentences.translation: 这些是将动物带离熟悉栖息地的长期移动；它们往往是线性的，而不是曲折的；

target_sen.target_sen: The biologist Hugh Dingle has identified five characteristics that apply, in varying degrees and combinations, to all migrations.
The biologist Hugh Dingle has identified five characteristics that apply, in varying degrees and combinations, to all migrations.

questions.title: To prepare for migration, animals are likely to
questions.analysis: 题干问的是动物在准备迁徙时可能会做什么。文章中明确提到，动物在准备迁徙时会进行过度进食（overfeeding）。因此，最直接对应的答案是 C: eat more than they need for immediate purposes。

central_sentences.sentence: they involve special behaviours concerning preparation (such as overfeeding) and arrival; they
central_sentences.translation: 它们涉及与准备（如过度进食）和到达相关的特殊行为；

target_sen.target_sen: They are prolonged movements that carry animals outside familiar habitats; they tend to be linear, not zigzaggy; they involve special behaviours concerning preparation (such as overfeeding) and arrival; they demand special allocations of energy.

questions.title: During migration, animals are unlikely to
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 A: be discouraged by difficulties。文章中明确提到迁徙的动物不会被挑战吓倒（undeterred by challenges），这与题干中的 "unlikely to be discouraged by difficulties" 是同义表达。因此，A 是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: migrating animals maintain an intense attentiveness to the greater mission, which keeps them undistracted by temptations and undeterred by challenges that would turn other animals aside.
central_sentences.translation: 迁徙中的动物保持对更大使命的高度专注，这使它们不会被诱惑分心，也不会被那些会让其他动物退缩的挑战所阻碍。

target_sen.target_sen: And one more: migrating animals maintain an intense attentiveness to the greater mission, which keeps them undistracted by temptations and undeterred by challenges that would turn other animals aside.

questions.title: Arctic terns illustrate migrating animals' ability to
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是E（ignore distractions），因为文章明确提到北极燕鸥在迁徙过程中抵制干扰，专注于其迁徙的目的。这一点与题干中的描述完全吻合。通过找到文章中的同义表达，理解题目和文章的对应关系，可以确保答案的准确性。

central_sentences.sentence: The arctic tern resists distraction because it is driven at that moment by an instinctive sense of something we humans find admirable: larger purpose.
central_sentences.translation: 北极燕鸥抵制干扰，因为它在那一刻被我们人类认为值得钦佩的本能驱使：更大的目标。

target_sen.target_sen: An arctic tern, on its 20,000 km flight from the extreme south of South America to the Arctic circle, will take no notice of a nice smelly herring offered from a bird-watcher's boat along the way.
The arctic tern resists distraction because it is driven at that moment by an instinctive sense of something we humans find admirable: larger purpose.

Reaching some gravelly coastline in the Arctic, upon which other arctic terns have converged, will serve its larger purpose as shaped by evolution: finding a place, a time, and a set of circumstances in which it can successfully hatch and rear offspring.
The arctic tern resists distraction because it is driven at that moment by an instinctive sense of something we humans find admirable: larger purpose.

groups.title: The migration of pronghorns
groups.desc:
Questions 23-26 
Complete the summary below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY from the text in each gap.
groups.collect: Pronghorns rely on their eyesight and $1205$to avoid predators. One particular population's summer habitat is a national park, and their winter home is on the $1206$ , where they go to avoid the danger presented by the snow at that time of year. However, their route between these two areas contains three $1207$. One problem is the construction of new homes in a narrow $1208$of land on the pronghorns' route.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“Pronghorns”在原文中对应“Pronghorn”，“eyesight”对应“distance vision”，“predators”在原文中直接出现。根据原文句子“Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators...”，可以看出“speed”是与“distance vision”并列的，且两者共同作用于躲避捕食者。因此，答案是“speed”。

central_sentences.sentence: Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators, traverse high, open shoulders of land, where they can see and run.
central_sentences.translation: 叉角羚依赖远视力和速度来保护自己免受捕食者的伤害，它们穿越高耸开阔的山坡，在那里它们可以看得远，跑得快。

target_sen.target_sen: Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators, traverse high, open shoulders of land, where they can see and run.
Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators, traverse high, open shoulders of land, where they can see and run.
Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators, traverse high, open shoulders of land, where they can see and run.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "plains"。题干问的是冬季栖息地在哪里，文章中明确提到这些动物在冬季会迁徙到平原（plains），以避开雪带来的危险。因此，答案是 "plains"。

central_sentences.sentence: One population, which spends the summer in the mountainous Grand Teton National Park of the western USA, follows a narrow route from its summer range in the mountains, across a river, and down onto the plains. Here they wait out the frozen months, feeding mainly on sagebrush blown clear of snow.
central_sentences.translation: 有一群羚羊在美国西部的大提顿国家公园的山区度过夏天，然后沿着一条狭窄的路线从山中的夏季栖息地出发，穿过一条河，下到平原上。它们在这里度过严寒的月份，主要以被风吹落积雪的山艾为食。

target_sen.target_sen: One population, which spends the summer in the mountainous Grand Teton National Park of the western USA, follows a narrow route from its summer range in the mountains, across a river, and down onto the plains.

One population, which spends the summer in the mountainous Grand Teton National Park of the western USA, follows a narrow route from its summer range in the mountains, across a river, and down onto the plains.
Here they wait out the frozen months, feeding mainly on sagebrush blown clear of snow.
Here they wait out the frozen months, feeding mainly on sagebrush blown clear of snow.

If they can't pass through each of the three during their spring migration, they can't reach their bounty of summer grazing; if they can't pass through again in autumn, escaping south onto those windblown plains, they are likely to die trying to overwinter in the deep snow.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“route”对应文章中的“migration route”，“contains”对应文章中的“constriction at”，“three”在两者中都是相同的数字。最关键的词是“bottlenecks”，它描述了路线中的狭窄部分。因此，正确答案是“bottlenecks”。

central_sentences.sentence: These pronghorn are notable for the invariance of their migration route and the severity of its constriction at three bottlenecks.
central_sentences.translation: 这些叉角羚因其迁徙路线的不变性和在三个瓶颈处的严重收缩而著称。

target_sen.target_sen: These pronghorn are notable for the invariance of their migration route and the severity of its constriction at three bottlenecks.
These pronghorn are notable for the invariance of their migration route and the severity of its constriction at three bottlenecks.

If they can't pass through each of the three during their spring migration, they can't reach their bounty of summer grazing; if they can't pass through again in autumn, escaping south onto those windblown plains, they are likely to die trying to overwinter in the deep snow.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "narrow" 对应原文中的 "only about 150 metres wide"，而 "land on the pronghorns' route" 对应 "corridor of open ground"。通过这些同义表达，我们可以确定答案所在的句子。原文中 "corridor" 和 "passageway" 都表示狭窄的通道，符合题干要求的地形特征，因此这两个词是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: At one of the bottlenecks, forested hills rise to form a V, leaving a corridor of open ground only about 150 metres wide, filled with private homes.
central_sentences.translation: 在其中一个瓶颈处，森林覆盖的山丘形成了一个V形，留下一条只有约150米宽的开阔地带作为通道，这里已经建满了私人住宅。

target_sen.target_sen: At one of the bottlenecks, forested hills rise to form a V, leaving a corridor of open ground only about 150 metres wide, filled with private homes.
At one of the bottlenecks, forested hills rise to form a V, leaving a corridor of open ground only about 150 metres wide, filled with private homes.

content.title: Preface to 'How the Other Half Thinks: Adventures in Mathematical Reasoning'
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了在一些困难的音乐作品和高级数学中，都存在着简单易懂的部分，甚至初学者也能够理解和演奏，而本书的每一章都展示了这种现象，使得每个读者都有机会参与数学体验，欣赏数学的美丽，并熟悉其逻辑而直观的思维方式。\n简短总结：本书的每一章都使用最多只有初等算术，有时甚至不需要算术，因此所有读者都有机会参与数学体验，欣赏数学的美丽，并熟悉其逻辑而直观的思维方式。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了作者写这本书的目的是为了让没有机会接触和欣赏真正数学的读者有机会欣赏数学思维方式，并揭示了一些有趣的发现背后的推理过程。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了人文科学和自然科学之间的差距可以通过培养直觉和分析能力来缩小或克服，并举例说明了一些人在不同领域展现出的多元才能。', 'D': '科学家们为了向非科学家解释他们的领域，写了很多书，但是他们不得不省略数学，尽管数学是他们理论的基础。读者只能作为一个心痒难耐的旁观者，而不是参与者，因为在科学的许多领域中，描述细节的适当语言是数学，无论是扩张的宇宙、亚原子粒子还是染色体。尽管科学理论的大致轮廓可以直观地勾勒出来，但当物理宇宙的一部分最终被理解时，它的描述通常看起来像数学教材中的一页。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了非数学领域的人也可以通过理解数学推理而取得很大的进步，本书提供了详细的细节来展示数学思维的风格，其中包括持续、逐步的分析、实验和洞察力，读者需要花费更多的时间来阅读这本书，并准备好铅笔和纸来验证论点和进行实验。', 'F': '这本书适合两类读者：一类是在五年级左右因为不愉快的经历而对数学失去兴趣的人，另一类是数学爱好者，他们会在整本书中找到很多新的内容。此外，这本书还适合那些想要提高分析能力的读者，因为许多职业，如法律和医学，需要进行深入、精确的分析。', 'G': '通过学习数学，特别是定理，可以培养分析复杂原则的能力，从而在医学和法律领域中取得成功。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text:
Occasionally, in some difficult musical compositions, there are beautiful, but easy parts—parts so simple a beginner could play them. So it is with mathematics as well. There are some discoveries in advanced mathematics that do not depend on specialized knowledge, not even on algebra, geometry, or trigonometry. Instead they may involve, at most, a little arithmetic, such as 'the sum of two odd numbers is even', and common sense. Each of the eight chapters in this book illustrates this phenomenon. Anyone can understand every step in the reasoning.
The thinking in each chapter uses at most only elementary arithmetic, and sometimes not even that. Thus all readers will have the chance to participate in a mathematical experience, to appreciate the beauty of mathematics, and to become familiar with its logical, yet intuitive, style of thinking.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text:
One of my purposes in writing this book is to give readers who haven't had the opportunity to see and enjoy real mathematics the chance to appreciate the mathematical way of thinking. I want to reveal not only some of the fascinating discoveries, but, more importantly, the reasoning behind them.
In that respect, this book differs from most books on mathematics written for the general public. Some present the lives of colorful mathematicians. Others describe important applications of mathematics. Yet others go into mathematical procedures, but assume that the reader is adept in using algebra.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: I hope this book will help bridge that notorious gap that separates the two cultures: the humanities and the sciences, or should I say the right brain (intuitive) and the left brain (analytical, numerical). As the chapters will illustrate, mathematics is not restricted to the analytical and numerical; intuition plays a significant role. The alleged gap can be narrowed or completely overcome by anyone, in part because each of us is far from using the full capacity of either side of the brain. To illustrate our human potential, I cite a structural engineer who is an artist, an electrical engineer who is an opera singer, an opera singer who published mathematical research, and a mathematician who publishes short stories.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics, although it provides the foundation of their theories. The reader must remain a tantalized spectator rather than an involved participant, since the appropriate language for describing the details in much of science is mathematics, whether the subject is expanding universe, subatomic particles, or chromosomes. Though the broad outline of a scientific theory can be sketched intuitively, when a part of the physical universe is finally understood, its description often looks like a page in a mathematics text.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Still, the non-mathematical reader can go far in understanding mathematical reasoning. This book presents the details that illustrate the mathematical style of thinking, which involves sustained, step-by-step analysis, experiments, and insights. You will turn these pages much more slowly than when reading a novel or a newspaper. It may help to have a pencil and paper ready to check claims and carry out experiments.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text:
As I wrote, I kept in mind two types of readers: those who enjoyed mathematics until they were turned off by an unpleasant episode, usually around fifth grade, and mathematics aficionados, who will find much that is new throughout the book.
This book also serves readers who simply want to sharpen their analytical skills. Many careers, such as law and medicine, require extended, precise analysis. Each chapter offers practice in following a sustained and closely argued line of thought. That mathematics can develop this skill is shown by these two testimonials:

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text:
A physician wrote, 'The discipline of analytical thought processes [in mathematics] prepared me extremely well for medical school. In medicine one is faced with a problem which must be thoroughly analyzed before a solution can be found. The process is similar to doing mathematics.'
A lawyer made the same point, 'Although I had no background in law—not even one political science course—I did well at one of the best law schools. I attribute much of my success there to having learned, through the study of mathematics, and, in particular, theorems, how to analyze complicated principles. Lawyers who have studied mathematics can master the legal principles in a way that most others cannot.'
I hope you will share my delight in watching as simple, even naive, questions lead to remarkable solutions and purely theoretical discoveries find unanticipated applications.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-34
Which section contains the following information?
Choose the correct answer.
NB You may use any letter more than once.

questions.title: a reference to books that assume a lack of mathematical knowledge
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "a reference to books that assume a lack of mathematical knowledge" 意思是提到假设读者缺乏数学知识的书籍。段落D中的句子 "Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics" 正好对应了这一点。这里的 "non-scientists" 和 "omit the mathematics" 表明这些书籍假设读者缺乏数学知识，因此段落D是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics, although it provides the foundation of their theories.
central_sentences.translation: 其他科学家撰写书籍向非科学家解释他们的领域，但不得不省略数学内容，尽管数学提供了他们理论的基础。

target_sen.target_sen: Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics, although it provides the foundation of their theories.
Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics, although it provides the foundation of their theories.

questions.title: the way in which this is not a typical book about mathematics
questions.analysis: 题干信息“the way in which this is not a typical book about mathematics”意思是“这本书与一般的数学书不同的方式”。段落B中的句子“In that respect, this book differs from most books on mathematics written for the general public”直接表达了这本书与大多数数学书不同的地方，因此答案是段落B。

central_sentences.sentence: In that respect, this book differs from most books on mathematics written for the general public.
central_sentences.translation: 在这方面，这本书与为普通大众写的大多数数学书不同。

target_sen.target_sen: In that respect, this book differs from most books on mathematics written for the general public.

questions.title: personal examples of being helped by mathematics
questions.analysis: 段落G中明确提到了两个人的个人例子，一个是医生，一个是律师。他们都描述了数学如何帮助了他们的职业发展。这些都是明确的个人例子，说明了数学对他们的帮助。因此，段落G与题干中的信息直接对应。

central_sentences.sentence: A physician wrote, 'The discipline of analytical thought processes [in mathematics] prepared me extremely well for medical school. In medicine one is faced with a problem which must be thoroughly analyzed before a solution can be found. The process is similar to doing mathematics.'
central_sentences.translation: 一位医生写道："数学中的分析思维过程训练为我的医学院学习做了极好的准备。在医学中，人们面对的问题必须经过深入分析才能找到解决方案。这个过程与做数学题很相似。"

central_sentences.sentence: A lawyer made the same point, 'Although I had no background in law - not even one political science course - I did well at one of the best law schools. I attribute much of my success there to having learned, through the study of mathematics, and, in particular, theorems, how to analyze complicated principles. Lawyers who have studied mathematics can master the legal principles in a way that most others cannot.'
central_sentences.translation: 一位律师也表达了相同的观点："尽管我没有法律背景——甚至没有上过一门政治学课程——我在最好的法学院之一表现出色。我将在那里的成功很大程度上归功于通过学习数学，特别是定理，学会了如何分析复杂的原理。学过数学的律师能以大多数人无法企及的方式掌握法律原理。"

target_sen.target_sen: A physician wrote, 'The discipline of analytical thought processes [in mathematics] prepared me extremely well for medical school.
A physician wrote, 'The discipline of analytical thought processes [in mathematics] prepared me extremely well for medical school.

syn_ary.synonym: A lawyer made the same point

target_sen.target_sen: A lawyer made the same point, 'Although I had no background in law - not even one political science course - I did well at one of the best law schools.
A lawyer made the same point, 'Although I had no background in law - not even one political science course - I did well at one of the best law schools.
A physician wrote, 'The discipline of analytical thought processes [in mathematics] prepared me extremely well for medical school.
A physician wrote, 'The discipline of analytical thought processes [in mathematics] prepared me extremely well for medical school.
I attribute much of my success there to having learned, through the study of mathematics, and, in particular, theorems, how to analyze complicated principles.
I attribute much of my success there to having learned, through the study of mathematics, and, in particular, theorems, how to analyze complicated principles.

questions.title: examples of people who each had abilities that seemed incompatible
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“examples of people who each had abilities that seemed incompatible”直接对应段落C中的例子，这些例子展示了具有看似不相容能力的人。段落C中的句子明确提到了一些人同时具备两种看似不相容的能力，如结构工程师兼艺术家、电气工程师兼歌剧演唱家等，这与题干信息完全吻合。

central_sentences.sentence: To illustrate our human potential, I cite a structural engineer who is an artist, an electrical engineer who is an opera singer, an opera singer who published mathematical research, and a mathematician who publishes short stories.
central_sentences.translation: 为了说明人类的潜力，我举例说明一位既是结构工程师又是艺术家的人，一位既是电气工程师又是歌剧演唱家的人，一位发表过数学研究的歌剧演唱家，以及一位发表短篇小说的数学家。

target_sen.target_sen: To illustrate our human potential, I cite a structural engineer who is an artist, an electrical engineer who is an opera singer, an opera singer who published mathematical research, and a mathematician who publishes short stories.

To illustrate our human potential, I cite a structural engineer who is an artist, an electrical engineer who is an opera singer, an opera singer who published mathematical research, and a mathematician who publishes short stories.

To illustrate our human potential, I cite a structural engineer who is an artist, an electrical engineer who is an opera singer, an opera singer who published mathematical research, and a mathematician who publishes short stories.

To illustrate our human potential, I cite a structural engineer who is an artist, an electrical engineer who is an opera singer, an opera singer who published mathematical research, and a mathematician who publishes short stories.

I hope this book will help bridge that notorious gap that separates the two cultures: the humanities and the sciences, or should I say the right brain (intuitive) and the left brain (analytical, numerical).
The alleged gap can be narrowed or completely overcome by anyone, in part because each of us is far from using the full capacity of either side of the brain.

questions.title: mention of different focuses of books about mathematics
questions.analysis: 段落B明确提到了关于数学书籍的不同侧重点，这与题干信息“mention of different focuses of books about mathematics”直接对应。段落B列举了几种不同类型的数学书籍：介绍数学家生活的书籍、描述数学应用的书籍和深入数学程序的书籍。这些内容正好回答了题干中的问题。

central_sentences.sentence: In that respect, this book differs from most books on mathematics written for the general public. Some present the lives of colorful mathematicians. Others describe important applications of mathematics. Yet others go into mathematical procedures, but assume that the reader is adept in using algebra.
central_sentences.translation: 在这方面，这本书与大多数为普通大众写的数学书不同。有些书介绍了富有色彩的数学家的生平。另一些书描述了数学的重要应用。还有一些书深入探讨数学程序，但假设读者精通代数。

target_sen.target_sen: In that respect, this book differs from most books on mathematics written for the general public.

syn_ary.synonym: books on mathematics written for the general public

target_sen.target_sen: In that respect, this book differs from most books on mathematics written for the general public.

questions.title: a contrast between reading this book and reading other kinds of publication
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落 E，因为段落 E 中明确提到了阅读这本书和阅读小说或报纸的不同之处，即对比。具体来说，段落 E 中的句子 "You will turn these pages much more slowly than when reading a novel or a newspaper." 直接指出了阅读这本书和阅读其他出版物的对比，这与题目中的信息 "a contrast between reading this book and reading other kinds of publication" 完全吻合。

central_sentences.sentence: You will turn these pages much more slowly than when reading a novel or a newspaper.
central_sentences.translation: 你翻阅这本书的速度会比阅读小说或报纸慢得多。

target_sen.target_sen: You will turn these pages much more slowly than when reading a novel or a newspaper.
You will turn these pages much more slowly than when reading a novel or a newspaper.
You will turn these pages much more slowly than when reading a novel or a newspaper.

questions.title: a claim that the whole of the book is accessible to everybody
questions.analysis: 题干中的“accessible to everybody”意思是“对所有人都可理解的”，在段落A中，明确提到“Anyone can understand every step in the reasoning”（任何人都可以理解推理的每一步）和“all readers will have the chance”（所有读者都有机会），这表明本书的内容对所有人都是可理解的。因此，段落A是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Anyone can understand every step in the reasoning.
central_sentences.translation: 任何人都能理解推理中的每一步。

central_sentences.sentence: Thus all readers will have the chance to participate in a mathematical experience, to appreciate the beauty of mathematics, and to become familiar with its logical, yet intuitive, style of thinking.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，所有读者都将有机会参与数学体验，欣赏数学之美，并熟悉其逻辑而直观的思维方式。

target_sen.target_sen: Anyone can understand every step in the reasoning.
Anyone can understand every step in the reasoning.
Anyone can understand every step in the reasoning.
Anyone can understand every step in the reasoning.
Thus all readers will have the chance to participate in a mathematical experience, to appreciate the beauty of mathematics, and to become familiar with its logical, yet intuitive, style of thinking.
Thus all readers will have the chance to participate in a mathematical experience, to appreciate the beauty of mathematics, and to become familiar with its logical, yet intuitive, style of thinking.

questions.title: a reference to different categories of intended readers of this book
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落F，因为段落F明确提到了“two types of readers”，这与题干中的“different categories of intended readers”完全对应。段落F详细描述了这两类读者的特点，符合题干要求。

central_sentences.sentence: As I wrote, I kept in mind two types of readers: those who enjoyed mathematics until they were turned off by an unpleasant episode, usually around fifth grade, and mathematics aficionados, who will find much that is new throughout the book.
central_sentences.translation: 在我写作时，我考虑了两类读者：那些曾经喜欢数学直到（通常在五年级左右）因为不愉快的经历而对数学失去兴趣的人，以及数学爱好者，他们将在整本书中发现许多新内容。

target_sen.target_sen: As I wrote, I kept in mind two types of readers: those who enjoyed mathematics until they were turned off by an unpleasant episode, usually around fifth grade, and mathematics aficionados, who will find much that is new throughout the book.

As I wrote, I kept in mind two types of readers: those who enjoyed mathematics until they were turned off by an unpleasant episode, usually around fifth grade, and mathematics aficionados, who will find much that is new throughout the book.

groups.desc:
Questions 35-40
Complete the sentences below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the text in each gap.
groups.collect:
Some areas of both music and mathematics are suitable for someone who is a$1217$.
It is sometimes possible to understand advanced mathematics using no more than a limited knowledge of$1218$.
The writer intends to show that mathematics requires$1219$thinking, as well as analytical skills.
Some books written by$1220$have had to leave out the mathematics that is central to their theories.
The writer advises non mathematical readers to perform$1221$while reading the book.
A lawyer found that studying$1222$helped even more than other areas of mathematics in the study of law.

questions.analysis: 答案是“beginner”，因为题干中的“suitable for someone who is a”在原文中对应的是“so simple a beginner could play them”。这句话明确指出，某些音乐和数学的部分简单到初学者也能理解和操作。因此，答案是“beginner”。

central_sentences.sentence: Occasionally, in some difficult musical compositions, there are beautiful, but easy parts - parts so simple a beginner could play them. So it is with mathematics as well.
central_sentences.translation: 有时，在一些困难的音乐作品中，也会有美丽但简单的部分——简单到初学者都能演奏的部分。数学也是如此。

target_sen.target_sen: Occasionally, in some difficult musical compositions, there are beautiful, but easy parts - parts so simple a beginner could play them.
So it is with mathematics as well.
There are some discoveries in advanced mathematics that do not depend on specialized knowledge, not even on algebra, geometry, or trigonometry.
Thus all readers will have the chance to participate in a mathematical experience, to appreciate the beauty of mathematics, and to become familiar with its logical, yet intuitive, style of thinking.
Occasionally, in some difficult musical compositions, there are beautiful, but easy parts - parts so simple a beginner could play them.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "advanced mathematics" 和 "limited knowledge" 在原文中分别对应 "advanced mathematics" 和 "a little"。原文提到理解高级数学时最多只需要一些算术知识（a little arithmetic），这与题干的要求完全一致。因此，正确答案是 "arithmetic"。

central_sentences.sentence: Instead they may involve, at most, a little arithmetic, such as 'the sum of two odd numbers is even', and common sense.
central_sentences.translation: 相反，它们最多只涉及一点算术，比如"两个奇数之和是偶数"，以及常识。

target_sen.target_sen: There are some discoveries in advanced mathematics that do not depend on specialized knowledge, not even on algebra, geometry, or trigonometry.
Instead they may involve, at most, a little arithmetic, such as 'the sum of two odd numbers is even', and common sense.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "mathematics requires" 对应原文中的 "mathematics is not restricted to"，而 "analytical skills" 对应 "analytical and numerical"。原文中提到 "intuition plays a significant role"，说明数学不仅需要分析技能，还需要直觉思维。因此，答案是 "intuitive"。

central_sentences.sentence: As the chapters will illustrate, mathematics is not restricted to the analytical and numerical; intuition plays a significant role.
central_sentences.translation: 正如各章节将会说明的那样，数学并不仅限于分析性和数字性思维；直觉也扮演着重要角色。

target_sen.target_sen: As the chapters will illustrate, mathematics is not restricted to the analytical and numerical; intuition plays a significant role.
As the chapters will illustrate, mathematics is not restricted to the analytical and numerical; intuition plays a significant role.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“books written by”对应原文中的“written books”，“leave out the mathematics”对应“omit the mathematics”，“central to their theories”对应“provides the foundation of their theories”。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案是“scientists”，因为原文明确指出是“Other scientists have written books”。

central_sentences.sentence: Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics, although it provides the foundation of their theories.
central_sentences.translation: 其他科学家已经写了一些书来向非科学家解释他们的领域，但必然不得不省略数学内容，尽管数学为他们的理论提供了基础。

target_sen.target_sen: Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics, although it provides the foundation of their theories.
Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics, although it provides the foundation of their theories.

syn_ary.synonym: provides the foundation of their theories

target_sen.target_sen: Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics, although it provides the foundation of their theories.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“advises”对应原文中的“may help”，“perform”对应“carry out”，“while reading the book”对应“as you read”。通过这些线索词的对应关系，可以定位到原文中的句子“准备好铅笔和纸来检查论点并进行实验可能会有所帮助”。因此，答案是“experiments”。

central_sentences.sentence: It may help to have a pencil and paper ready to check claims and carry out experiments.
central_sentences.translation: 准备好铅笔和纸来检查论点并进行实验可能会有帮助。

target_sen.target_sen: It may help to have a pencil and paper ready to check claims and carry out experiments.

syn_ary.synonym: It may help to have a pencil and paper ready

target_sen.target_sen: It may help to have a pencil and paper ready to check claims and carry out experiments.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "theorems"。题干中的 "A lawyer found that studying" 对应文章中的 "A lawyer made the same point"。题干中的 "helped even more than other areas of mathematics" 对应文章中的 "in particular, theorems"。文章明确指出，律师特别强调了定理在他法律学习中的重要性，因此答案是 "theorems"。

central_sentences.sentence: A lawyer made the same point, 'Although I had no background in law - not even one political science course - I did well at one of the best law schools. I attribute much of my success there to having learned, through the study of mathematics, and, in particular, theorems, how to analyze complicated principles.
central_sentences.translation: 一位律师也表达了同样的观点，"尽管我没有法律背景——甚至没有上过一门政治学课程——我在最好的法学院之一表现出色。我将我的成功很大程度上归功于通过学习数学，特别是定理，学会了如何分析复杂的原理。"

target_sen.target_sen: A lawyer made the same point, 'Although I had no background in law - not even one political science course - I did well at one of the best law schools.
I attribute much of my success there to having learned, through the study of mathematics, and, in particular, theorems, how to analyze complicated principles.
A physician wrote, 'The discipline of analytical thought processes [in mathematics] prepared me extremely well for medical school.
The process is similar to doing mathematics.'
I attribute much of my success there to having learned, through the study of mathematics, and, in particular, theorems, how to analyze complicated principles.
Lawyers who have studied mathematics can master the legal principles in a way that most others cannot.'
